mechanism_narrative: A 5-month growth acceleration in the US GDP signals increased consumer and business spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of instances where GDP growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index signals reduced capacity utilization, leading to decreased supply chain bottlenecks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 50% of instances where industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals increased labor market slack, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of instances where the unemployment rate rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of instances where the federal funds rate rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month growth acceleration in the US GDP and a 2-month rise in the housing market signal increased consumer and business spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of instances where both GDP growth accelerates and the housing market rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the industrial production index and a 3-month rise in the unemployment rate signal reduced capacity utilization and increased labor market slack, leading to decreased supply chain bottlenecks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of instances where both industrial production declines and the unemployment rate rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate and a 3-month decline in the housing market signal a tightening monetary cycle and decreased consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of instances where both the federal funds rate rises and the housing market declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month growth acceleration in the US GDP and a 2-month rise in the industrial production index signal increased consumer and business spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of instances where GDP growth accelerates and industrial production rises.
